results.taskDescription: Fetch top 15 HN posts and write a paragraph-style summary categorizing them by topic. Tests substantive synthesis from observation data.
results.output:
# Today on Hacker News

## AI and Technology
The top stories today showcase significant developments in artificial intelligence and technology. Koshy John's thought-provoking article "AI should elevate your thinking, not replace it" has garnered substantial attention with 450 comments. This piece highlights the importance of AI as a tool to enhance human capabilities rather than simply replacing them. The article also discusses practical applications and future implications of AI in our daily lives.

## Business and Entrepreneurship
In the business realm, two notable stories stand out. First, "I bought Friendster for $30k – Here's what I'm doing with it" has become a viral hit on Hacker News, receiving an impressive 917 points and 464 comments. This story details the purchase of the defunct social media platform Friendster and outlines plans to revive its legacy. Another interesting business-related post is "Men Who Stare at Walls," which explores unconventional approaches to problem-solving in the tech industry.

## Hardware and Software Development
The hardware and software development community has been active today, with several notable posts. The most discussed technical story is "Fully Featured Audio DSP Firmware for the Raspberry Pi Pico," which has attracted 19 comments and showcases impressive work on audio processing capabilities. Another interesting post is "Show HN: OSS Agent I built topped the TerminalBench on Gemini-3-flash-preview," highlighting innovative achievements in terminal performance optimization.

## Other Notable Stories
Beyond these main categories, several other stories have caught attention. The "It's OK to abandon your side-project (2024)" article has sparked a meaningful discussion about project management and personal goals, with 58 comments. Additionally, the "Self-updating screenshots" post has generated interest in automated screenshot technology, while "TurboQuant: A first-principles walkthrough" offers insights into advanced financial modeling techniques.

These diverse stories reflect the broad range of interests within the Hacker News community, from technical innovations to business ventures and personal development topics.
